data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children once more; third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260514T032933-ca0f5fef/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing child IDs, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a success. Do not mix implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260514T032933-ca0f5fef/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260514T032933-ca0f5fef/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

To request orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260514T032933-ca0f5fef/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260514T032933-ca0f5fef/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-c3219628">
    <title>work: report live workers only for the requested project</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
Operators and agents asking "is the worker still working beads?" or "how is the queue progressing?" can receive the status of a DDx worker from the wrong repository. In the observed incident, the user was asking about the Niflheim queue, but the agent ran a global `ps aux | grep -E "(ddx work|ddx try)"` and reported a DDx repo worker on `ddx-bbdbc903`. This is actively harmful: it makes project A look busy because project B has a worker, and it hides whether project A has stalled.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `.agents/skills/ddx/reference/status.md:22-31` tells agents how to check queue state with `ddx bead ready`, `ddx bead blocked`, `ddx bead status`, and `ddx bead list`, but it gives no required project-root confirmation or project-scoped live-worker check.
- `.agents/skills/ddx/reference/status.md:82-99` warns not to mix health/status commands, but does not warn against global process scans or require that worker reports match the requested project root.
- `.agents/skills/ddx/reference/work.md:143-159` documents `ddx work` and `ddx try`, but does not document a safe way to ask "which live workers belong to this project?".
- `cli/cmd/work.go:76` exposes `--project`, and `cli/cmd/work.go:144-165` includes `project_root` in terminal/JSON loop output, but DDx has no corresponding `ddx work status` / `ddx worker status` local CLI that lists live worker processes for the current project and filters out other repositories.
- `cli/cmd/server_workers.go:13-175` provides server-backed worker listing/logging, but that only helps when the local server worker registry is running; it does not solve ordinary inline `ddx work` shells that operators check from a terminal.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a project-scoped live-worker status surface and update skill guidance so agents cannot accidentally answer with cross-project workers.

Recommended implementation shape:
- Add `ddx work status` (or an equivalent `ddx worker status`) that defaults to the current git/project root, accepts `--project`, and lists only live `ddx work` / `ddx try` processes whose process cwd, `--project`, execution worktree, or durable worker record maps to that root.
- Include `--all-projects` for explicit global inspection. The default must never report workers from other repositories.
- Output should include project root, pid, age, command, active bead ID when inferable, execution worktree or bundle path when inferable, and a clear `no live workers for <project>` result when none match.
- Update `.agents/skills/ddx/reference/status.md` and `.agents/skills/ddx/reference/work.md` to require: first resolve the requested project root; then use the project-scoped status command; never use raw global `ps | grep ddx work` as evidence without filtering by project root; always name the project whose worker status is being reported.
- If a live worker is found for another project while answering a scoped question, mention it only as an explicitly out-of-scope process, not as proof that the requested project is progressing.

NON-SCOPE
Do not build a full multi-project scheduler. Do not require the DDx server to be running. Do not change bead claiming semantics. Do not kill or restart existing workers. Do not remove the server-backed worker APIs.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. `TestWorkStatusDefaultsToCurrentProjectOnly` or equivalent starts/fixtures workers for two project roots and proves `ddx work status --json` from project A reports only project A workers.
2. `TestWorkStatusAllProjectsShowsExplicitGlobalView` or equivalent proves `ddx work status --all-projects --json` includes workers from multiple project roots and labels each with `project_root`.
3. `TestWorkStatusNoLiveWorkersNamesProjectRoot` or equivalent proves the empty state says no live workers for the resolved project root, rather than falling back to unrelated processes.
4. `TestWorkStatusInfersBeadAndExecutionWorktree` or equivalent proves a live inline `ddx work`/`ddx try` process exposes pid, age, command, project_root, and active bead ID when the command line or execution worktree contains one.
5. `.agents/skills/ddx/reference/status.md`, `.claude/skills/ddx/reference/status.md`, and `cli/internal/skills/ddx/reference/status.md` document the project-scoped worker-status rule and forbid unfiltered global `ps | grep ddx work` evidence.
6. `.agents/skills/ddx/reference/work.md`, `.claude/skills/ddx/reference/work.md`, and `cli/internal/skills/ddx/reference/work.md` document the safe worker-status command and the `--all-projects` escape hatch.
7. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... -run "TestWorkStatus.*|TestServerWorkers.*" -count=1` passes.
8. `ddx skills check .agents/skills/ddx .claude/skills/ddx cli/internal/skills/ddx` passes, or the repository-equivalent skill validation command passes.
9. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>area:work, area:cli, area:skills, kind:bug, phase:build, reliability, operator-ux</labels>
    <metadata spec-id="FEAT-010" base-rev="26fec7ee76f04aba43cce8187d305164628327db" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260514T032933-ca0f5fef"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <ref id="FEAT-010" path="docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md">Feature: Task Execution</ref>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>
